title: DashMap lock released before broadcast sends — correct concurrency design
description: state.rs broadcast() first collects 'let targets: Vec<ClientTx> = self.inner.clients.iter()...collect()' then releases the DashMap shard locks, then drives all sends concurrently via join_all. A contributor might simplify this by iterating the DashMap and calling tx.send().await inside the iterator. Do not do that. Awaiting while holding a DashMap shard lock would deadlock if any handler that's waiting for the lock is also blocked on the send. The collect-then-send pattern is the correct way to hold DashMap for the minimum time and never await while holding it.
close_reason: informational review note — design confirmed correct, no action needed
